title: Shaq

jokes.text: Where's the bottom of the broccoli? Can somebody tell me what they did to the bottom of the broccoli? I like the bottom, it's crunchy.
jokes.setup: Larry examining broccoli at dinner
jokes.punchline: Obsessing over missing broccoli stems with misplaced indignation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David character comedy - taking a non-issue and treating it as a grave injustice with genuine outrage

jokes.text: Is there one person at this table who can eat cauliflower? What is it? Who put this on earth, this vegetable?
jokes.setup: Continuing vegetable complaints
jokes.punchline: Questioning cauliflower's right to exist with cosmic bewilderment
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry misanthropy directed at vegetables - slightly less inspired than the broccoli bit

jokes.text: You want to switch glasses with me, is that it? I like that whole Clark Kent thing you got happening there.
jokes.setup: Jeremy wanting to ask Larry something
jokes.punchline: Larry assumes it's about glasses and makes Superman reference
jokes.explanation: Mild misdirection joke - decent character beat but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: I love the indebted part, believe me. Don't get me wrong.
jokes.setup: Jeremy saying he'd be indebted forever for the recommendation
jokes.punchline: Larry openly admitting he enjoys having people owe him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry - saying the quiet part loud about enjoying power over others

jokes.text: You're not a Lakers fan are you? You're a Knicks fan. Table for one.
jokes.setup: Discussing Lakers season starting
jokes.punchline: Insulting Knicks fan by suggesting they eat alone
jokes.explanation: Snappy sports rivalry joke with good economy - 'table for one' is a nice put-down

jokes.text: I get so much satisfaction out of seeing 19,000 people disappointed when they lose
jokes.setup: Larry explaining why he'll root against Lakers
jokes.punchline: Admitting he enjoys mass disappointment
jokes.explanation: Excellent Larry character work - the specific number '19,000' and gleeful malice is perfectly crafted

jokes.text: I don't know, how much was this? $60, or what, I don't know, $80? What did you have? I mean, we just had some turkey. What did we have, turkey and a glass of wine? I don't even know what to put down here. I mean, I'm.... $90, how's that, is that good? Is $90 good? $110? $115, what'd you have?
jokes.setup: Larry offering to pay for dinner
jokes.punchline: Keeps increasing the amount when no one stops him, getting increasingly flustered
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating cringe comedy - Larry's social anxiety manifesting as compulsive overpayment. The repetitive structure builds perfectly

jokes.text: I go out to dinner, I wind up with a homework assignment.
jokes.setup: Larry reflecting on the evening
jokes.punchline: Comparing the birthday book request to homework
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Larry's worldview - social obligations as unwanted assignments. Great economy.

jokes.text: What was all that goodbye business? That was really embarrassing. I wanted to remind you to say goodbye.
jokes.setup: Cheryl prompting Larry to say goodbye
jokes.punchline: Larry being embarrassed by being reminded of basic manners
jokes.explanation: Establishes the goodbye running gag that will pay off throughout the episode

jokes.text: I just kept putting money down. Why didn't they stop me? Because they didn't stop me.
jokes.setup: Cheryl asking why he kept adding money
jokes.punchline: Larry's circular logic - blaming others for not stopping his compulsive behavior
jokes.explanation: Great callback with perfect Larry logic - refusing personal responsibility through absurd reasoning

jokes.text: You don't know what the hell you're talking about... 'Mr. Expert in somebody else's field.'
jokes.setup: Argument about whether Arabia is a country
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic title for Larry being wrong
jokes.explanation: Good character moment establishing the in-laws' dynamic with Larry - the formal sarcasm works well

jokes.text: Larry, don't you say goodnight?
jokes.setup: Larry leaving without saying goodnight
jokes.punchline: Another reminder about basic manners
jokes.explanation: Running gag development - the repetition is building the pattern for later payoffs

jokes.text: Honey, please don't yell at my parents like that. I wasn't yelling, I was helping.
jokes.setup: Larry being loud during Scattergories
jokes.punchline: Larry's complete lack of self-awareness about his aggressive 'helping'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry self-delusion - genuinely believes his aggressive behavior is helpful

jokes.text: Dear LA County Museum. This is a letter of recommendation for a guy named... a guy name Jeremy, who's somewhat of an acquaintance. And he draws beautiful concentric circles... and geometric figures that are quite moving.
jokes.setup: Larry reading his recommendation letter draft
jokes.punchline: Extremely underwhelming and vague description of Jeremy's art
jokes.explanation: Brilliant writing - Larry's complete inability to write professionally, reducing art to 'concentric circles'

jokes.text: By the way, I could make those triangles and those circles. You know, is that art? I mean, if I could do it, is that art?
jokes.setup: Looking at Jeremy's geometric art
jokes.punchline: Using his own incompetence as the measure of artistic value
jokes.explanation: Excellent Larry logic - if he can do it, it can't be good. Perfect character integration and relatable art criticism

jokes.text: I'll find out his last name and write back to you.
jokes.setup: Continuing to read his terrible recommendation letter
jokes.punchline: Admitting he doesn't even know Jeremy's full name
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the terrible recommendation - the casual admission of not knowing basic details

jokes.text: Shaq loves your show. Really? Yeah, loves it.
jokes.setup: Shaq's agent mentioning Shaq loves Seinfeld
jokes.punchline: Sets up the irony for later when Larry will injure Shaq
jokes.explanation: Important setup that becomes deeply ironic later - well-structured dramatic irony

jokes.text: You know, you ought to go to a doctor. Really? Yeah. You know that guy right over there? That's Dr. Wiggins, that's who he was talking about.
jokes.setup: Jeff showing Larry something on his back
jokes.punchline: Convenient coincidence of Lakers doctor being right there
jokes.explanation: Plot setup more than pure comedy - moves story forward but not particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: Next time you're in a big hurry... why don't you write me a bunch of shit for free?
jokes.setup: Larry asking for free medical advice
jokes.punchline: Doctor's perfect analogy turning Larry's profession against him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant comeback - perfect parallel structure and devastating logic. The doctor turns Larry's own profession into the punchline

jokes.text: Is this fucking unbelievable? This is unbelievable.
jokes.setup: Seeing their incredible Lakers seats
jokes.punchline: Larry's over-the-top amazement at the seating
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction comedy - not particularly clever but shows Larry's character

jokes.text: Yeah, I'll ask him, with his eight rings, that Larry David's upset.
jokes.setup: Larry questioning Phil Jackson's coaching decisions
jokes.punchline: Jeff sarcastically suggesting they question a legendary coach
jokes.explanation: Good reality check humor - Jeff puncturing Larry's delusions with specific facts

jokes.text: They said you tripped Shaquille O'Neal, now he'll be out for two months. You mean I tripped him? They said Larry David?
jokes.setup: Cheryl explaining Larry's on the news
jokes.punchline: Larry's disbelief at being specifically named and blamed
jokes.explanation: Good cringe development - Larry's horror at being publicly identified escalates nicely

jokes.text: Yeah, I did have a motive. I wanted to kill him, I had a motive.
jokes.setup: Reporter suggesting Larry had a motive as Knicks fan
jokes.punchline: Larry sarcastically confirming the ridiculous accusation
jokes.explanation: Solid sarcastic response - Larry making the accusation even more absurd through agreement

jokes.text: Don't you say goodnight?
jokes.setup: Larry leaving room during phone chaos
jokes.punchline: Third reminder about saying goodnight
jokes.explanation: Running gag building momentum - the repetition is setting up for bigger payoffs later

jokes.text: Asshole! Yeah, you, it's all your fault! Yeah, asshole!
jokes.setup: Lakers fans recognizing Larry outside
jokes.punchline: Public humiliation and anger from random strangers
jokes.explanation: Escalates consequences effectively but not particularly clever - functional cringe comedy

jokes.text: What can I do? Can I do anything? Buy the team.
jokes.setup: Larry asking how he can make it up to Jeff
jokes.punchline: Jeff's impossible demand escalating the consequences
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke with nice economy - Jeff's hyperbolic demand is perfectly delivered

jokes.text: No goodbye?
jokes.setup: Larry leaving office abruptly
jokes.punchline: Even strangers now expect Larry to say goodbye
jokes.explanation: Excellent running gag development - the joke has spread beyond family to random people

jokes.text: No goodbye?
jokes.setup: Larry leaving his office abruptly
jokes.punchline: Even his assistant notices his goodbye problem
jokes.explanation: Running gag spreading to more characters. Everyone notices Larry's social failings.

jokes.text: Don't you say goodbye?
jokes.setup: Parents leaving after Larry's chaos
jokes.punchline: Fifth iteration of the goodbye reminder
jokes.explanation: Running gag paying off - the repetition is now clearly established as a Larry character flaw

jokes.text: I was gonna do a basketball with.... I was going to forge some of the Lakers' signatures on it....
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his planned birthday gift
jokes.punchline: The tragic irony of planning Lakers memorabilia after destroying relationship with team
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic comedy - Larry's planned gift becomes impossible due to his own actions. Great structure.

jokes.text: One of the vanilla bullshit things. You know, do whatever you want. Some vanilla bullshit, latte cappa-thing. Whatever you got, I don't care.
jokes.setup: Larry ordering coffee at Starbucks
jokes.punchline: His contemptuous dismissal of coffee complexity
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry disdain for pretention - 'vanilla bullshit' is perfectly dismissive

jokes.text: Just too enthusiastic, and I miss the old Larry. Do you? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Larry being nice to coffee shop worker
jokes.punchline: Cheryl preferring Larry's usual rudeness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character reversal - even his wife prefers the authentic asshole Larry to fake-nice Larry

jokes.text: 'Cause I wrote some really laudatory things about your work... with the geometric shapes... the triangles and the trapezoids and all of that.
jokes.setup: Jeremy withdrawing the recommendation request
jokes.punchline: Larry's continued reduction of art to basic shapes
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier art commentary - Larry's consistent inability to discuss art sophisticatedly

jokes.text: Coffee on me! Ladies and gentlemen... free coffee and donuts and Danish and bagels. Anything you want. On me, I got it, everybody, come on.
jokes.setup: Larry's isolation and guilt
jokes.punchline: Desperate attempt to buy strangers' affection
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's desperation - the grand gesture feels authentic to his psychology

jokes.text: Would you mind taking a quick glance at this thing on my back? Tell me if it's anything that I should be concerned about. Sure, turn around.
jokes.setup: Larry asking another doctor for free medical advice
jokes.punchline: This doctor actually helps, contrasting with Dr. Wiggins
jokes.explanation: Nice callback structure - this doctor's helpfulness makes Dr. Wiggins look worse in retrospect

jokes.text: This is a solar keratosis. It's benign, it's from the sun. Really? I wouldn't worry about it. It's nothing.
jokes.setup: Doctor examining Larry's back
jokes.punchline: Professional diagnosis taking seconds, proving Dr. Wiggins was just being difficult
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback payoff - proves Larry was right that it would take seconds and validates his frustration

jokes.text: The whole world knows peanut butter is a dairy product. What are you saying? Dairy is from a cow. Peanuts are not a dairy product. Peanut butter, moo.
jokes.setup: Scattergories argument about peanut butter
jokes.punchline: Shaq's confident wrongness including 'moo' sound effect
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - Shaq's 'moo' sound effect to support his wrong argument is comedy gold

jokes.text: Absolutely, butter. Thank you. A man that has some sense.
jokes.setup: Larry agreeing peanut butter is dairy
jokes.punchline: Shaq praising Larry for being wrong, cementing their reconciliation through shared ignorance
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Larry bonds with Shaq through mutual wrongness. The irony is beautiful.

jokes.text: Why would you cheat at Scattergories? A doctor.
jokes.setup: Discovering the doctor cheated at the game
jokes.punchline: The profession making the cheating more absurd
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - the doctor's profession makes the petty cheating even more ridiculous

jokes.text: Don't you say goodbye? Notice how no one ever says goodbye anymore? Have you noticed that? It's unbelievable.
jokes.setup: Doctor leaving without saying goodbye
jokes.punchline: Larry finally articulating the goodbye issue as a societal problem, not his personal failing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant running gag payoff - Larry finally makes the goodbye issue about others, not himself. Perfect character moment and meta-commentary.

jokes.text: Don't you say goodbye? Notice how no one ever says goodbye anymore? Have you noticed that? It's unbelievable.
jokes.setup: Doctor leaves without saying goodbye
jokes.punchline: Larry finally getting validation about his goodbye theory
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the goodbye running gag. Larry finally gets someone to agree with him.

jokes.text: If you pop in The Contest, I'll let bygones be bygones... and I forgot that you tripped me.
jokes.setup: Larry giving Shaq Seinfeld tapes
jokes.punchline: Shaq forgiving everything for his favorite episode
jokes.explanation: Nice resolution callback - the Seinfeld fandom pays off as conflict resolution mechanism

jokes.text: He's not a doctor? Sixth floor, Psychiatric.
jokes.setup: The helpful 'doctor' offering Larry a ride
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's actually a psychiatric patient
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection ending - the one doctor who helped Larry wasn't actually a doctor. Brilliant callback structure.

tmdb_overview: Larry is villainized for accidentally tripping Shaquille O'Neal at a Lakers game, but the incident ironically ends up bringing him good luck.
